description: Use this API routine to run the second release of the inertial and governor response power flow calculation. This API routine was first introduced in release 33.0.0. ierr = inlf_2(options, ifile)

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: An array of eight elements specifying solution options. (input).

parameters.name: ifile
parameters.description: Name of unit inertia and governor data file; blank for none. (input; blank by default).

options.description: solution type (0 by default).
inertial.
governor response.
tap adjustment flag (tap adjustment option setting by default).
solution type default (inertial => disable; governor => stepping).
disable.
enable stepping adjustment.
enable direct adjustment.
phase shift adjustment flag (phase shift adjustment option setting by default).
solution type default (inertial => disable; governor => enable).
disable.
enable.
dc tap adjustment flag (dc tap adjustment option setting by default).
solution type default (inertial => disable; governor => enable).
disable.
enable.
switched shunt adjustment flag (switched shunt adjustment option setting by default).
solution type default (inertial => enable; governor => enable).
disable.
enable.
enable continuous mode, disable discrete mode.
generator var limit flag (-1 (inertial) or 99 (governor) by default).
apply var limits immediately.
apply var limits on iteration n (or sooner if mismatch gets small).
ignore var limits.
induction motor treatment flag; applied when an induction motor fails to solve due to low terminal voltage (0 by default).
stall.
trip.
missing active power limits flag (only used in governor response power flow) (0 by default).
pre-PSSE-29 approach (0.0 to 1.0 pu for machines with no Unit Inertia and Governor Data File record read; working case values for machines with PMAX defaulted on ECDI data record).
0.0 to 1.0 pu for both categories of machines.
working case values for both categories of machines.

error_codes.description: bus type code and series element status inconsistencies.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
